description: The declared release-gate thresholds for ADR-051 §7. Split into (1) ACTIVE local-regression tolerances that gate day-to-day changes against the committed provisional baseline (scripts/adr051_slo_gate.py), (2) the production SLOs whose absolute numbers are measured on the declared hardware class via the GKE fleet run and enforced by scripts/slo_gate.py, and (3) the ALWAYS-ON absolute correctness gates that are hardware-independent.

local_regression.rationale: Hardware-independent: catches a local change that regresses the serving path against its own recorded baseline, without pretending a dev box states production capacity. Enforced by scripts/adr051_slo_gate.py against a fresh run of the baseline anchor config.

tolerances.comment: A fresh anchor run must sustain >= 85% of baseline throughput and stay within the stated multiples of baseline added-latency percentiles. Bands are wider on the tail (p999) than the median because the tail is noisier on a co-located box; tighten them once a dedicated loadgen removes that noise.

per_core_scaling.comment: NOT gated locally: the 1->N curve is non-authoritative on a single box (co-located loadgen contends for cores). Recorded only; the authoritative curve lives in production_slo below.

production_slo.superseded_note: The measurements below were taken 2026-07-10 on the DELETED object/JCS carrier at the v1 envelope (concurrency 64, 2000 requests) — HALF the concurrency and a QUARTER of the requests of the local baseline anchor, so the two were never comparable. They do NOT describe the current RFC 9421 serving path and are retained only as a historical record. The production floors below are PENDING re-measurement on the GKE fleet under the v2 canonical envelope (RFC 9421 carrier, concurrency 128 / 8000 requests — the SAME envelope the local baseline uses). Do not treat these targets as active until that re-measurement lands. Run the local baseline first (docs/bench/adr-051-baseline-local.json, already on RFC 9421) before spending on GKE.
production_slo.measured_on: 2026-07-10 (SUPERSEDED); GKE us-central1-a cluster mcp-re-fleet; tls_load_harness_bench (async_serve, object/JCS carrier) as a K8s Job on a dedicated node; cold TLS1.3-mTLS, concurrency 64, 2000 requests/run; MCP_RE_LOADGEN_CORES 1 then 8; in-process echo inner, in-memory replay. Enforced by scripts/slo_gate.py.
production_slo.hardware_note: The DECLARED floor is the WEAKER of the two measured classes (e2-standard-8); a run on that class or better clears these targets (c3-standard-8, measured faster, also clears them). Floors ~= 0.64x measured median throughput; latency ceilings ~= 1.5-1.8x measured tail, with margin for run-to-run variance.

per_core_scaling.comment: achieved_N_core_throughput / (single_core_throughput * N) must be >= linear_tolerance_min. Floor set below the WEAKER measured factor (c3 0.662, e2 0.718) with margin.

measurements.note: SUPERSEDED historical record — object/JCS carrier, v1 envelope (concurrency 64, 2000 requests), GKE 2026-07-10. Retained for provenance only; NOT the RFC 9421 numbers. Raw measured baselines (no fabrication). throughput = verified responses/sec; latency in microseconds; scaling = tput_8 / (tput_1 * 8).

absolute_gates.kind: hardware-independent correctness gates — always required, every release

gates.name: replay-race
gates.requirement: N concurrent submissions of the same signed request across cores and across replicas yield EXACTLY ONE Fresh and N-1 Replay.

gates.name: bounded-drain
gates.requirement: Graceful drain joins all in-flight requests within the bounded grace window; zero abandoned requests.
